steps.title: Make a table from the point and slope
steps.content: The point $(0,-8)$ is on the graph. With slope $3$, moving one unit right raises $y$ by $3$, so another point is $(1,-5)$.

steps.title: Fit the line
steps.content: Run $y_1 \sim mx_1+b$. Desmos returns $m=3$ and $b=-8$, giving $f(x)=3x-8$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content: All four choices with $3x$ have the right slope only if their constant term is correct. Choice A has intercept $0$, choice C has intercept $5$, and choice D has intercept $11$. Choice B has intercept $-8$, so choice B is correct.
